description_long: This is a makeshift wooden fence constructed from several dark brown planks arranged in a haphazard, crisscrossed pattern. The fence features five vertical posts, each with a pointed top, and three horizontal rails, two of which are straight while one is angled diagonally for added reinforcement. The wood appears rough and unfinished, with visible grain and slight irregularities in the plank shapes and edges. The structure is thin when viewed from the side, indicating that the planks are not very thick. There are no visible decorative elements or hardware, giving the fence a utilitarian, possibly temporary or barricade-like appearance. The overall look suggests it could be used to block off an area in an urban environment, such as a construction site or alleyway.
description: A rough, dark brown wooden fence with pointed posts and crisscrossed planks, giving it a makeshift, barricade-like appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing five pointed vertical posts and three horizontal rails, with one rail set diagonally for reinforcement.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the overlapping arrangement of the wooden planks.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same pointed posts and crisscrossed rails, but with slight differences in plank overlap.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the structure and the staggered arrangement of the planks.
